generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE contracts.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) match the expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use AGREE logical operators like `and`, `or`, `not`, and `=>` for contract logic.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence (e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`).
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on contract corrections.
Do not use implementation-level details in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features, connections)
AGREE assumption/guarantee placement in component types vs. implementations
